programme.desc: Nick Robinson has a conversation with, not an interrogation of, the people who shape our political thinking about what shaped theirs.

programme.title: The Boss

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: Back in L'Isle-sur-la-Sorgue, a mysterious pair of metal boots catch Jon's eye. Meanwhile, Lucy, who literally wrote the book on mid-century chairs, buys with her heart rather than her head.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers the secrets of Cornwall's Tintagel, famed as the legendary birthplace of King Arthur. Ben discovers Tintagel was potentially more important than London a thousand years ago, based on recent evidence unearthed at an archaeological dig overseen by English Heritage. It was found that over 2,000 people were living here which, at the time, which would have made it the largest settlement anywhere of its kind in Britain.

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: With the help of 3D scanning technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the watery wonderland of Venice. They uncover how a city built in a swamp became one of the most powerful in medieval Europe, and dive into its canals to experience how the city remains standing.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and antiques experts Michael Baggott and Kate Bateman visit Dulwich College in London. Michael falls in love with a Chinese censor, Kate is enamoured by a charming collection of bronze animals and Paul makes the short journey to Dulwich Picture Gallery, where he is privileged to see one of the best collections of European old masters in the world.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Josh Jones, Sarah Keyworth, Melvin Odoom and Kimberly Wyatt test their general knowledge skills on this week's Richard Osman's House of Games: Redemption Week.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton test Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Gladiator Apollo, on their knowledge of the UK. Today's stop is Wrexham.

programme.title: The Wild Gardener
programme.desc: Working through the winter, Colin reflects on his journey so far and plans the busy spring and summer months ahead. With most of the heavy lifting done, Colin will now use a gentle hand to maintain the garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With a new season in the garden to look forward to, Monty revisits some inspirational stories from across 2025.

programme.desc: In Silicon Valley, plans are underway for massive changes in the workplace prompting fears that millions of people will be replaced by robots and AI. So, will this future bring freedom from work or a dystopia where workers are impoverished?

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A cohort of junior doctors join the midwives on their district rounds, including Timothy Turner, while Violet turns her hand to midwifery.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's judging day, and only one chef can go through to represent Scotland. Bafta-winning actress Katherine Parkinson helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs.

programme.title: The Zero Line: Inside Russia's War
programme.desc: Russian soldiers and civilians film with the BBC in secret, exposing how men on the front line are executed and forced to fight against their will, and how dissent is crushed at home.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: A preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piris are in the final of the synchronised swimming competition and are beyond excited. All except Pancake, who worries he will spoil their chances of glory with his two left flippers.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Volcanic smoke blocks all three suns, and Big Lizard becomes worryingly cold. Cosima needs a red-hot solution.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy, an immature and carefree weasel, suddenly becomes the adoptive parent of two ducklings he accidentally hatched.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: When a witch struggles to master her new wand, Twinkle decides to have a go, only to make her friends vanish. She must solo quest to find them.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The extraordinary animals from a tablet game come to life after the device explodes.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers that the Lemmings have replaced his TV with a fake one. Furious, he drives the Lemmings out of their hiding place where they'd hoped to watch their favourite cartoon.

programme.title: Game on Grandparents
programme.desc: Samba de Amigo takes centre stage. Two teams, guided by pro gamer Jelly, dance their way towards triumph. Which team will shimmy to success?

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe go out of this world as Callum transforms them into UFO taxi drivers.

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Nitro hires a fake image guru and, with help from the Gladiator producers, tell Viper and Phantom they need new looks as a prancing unicorn and a lassoing cowboy!

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Packed full of amazing celeb guests, epic challenges and lols galore!

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With a new season in the garden to look forward to, Monty revisits some inspirational stories from across 2025.

programme.title: The Great Caruso
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the career of legendary tenor Enrico Caruso, from his days spent singing on street corners in order to survive, to his apprenticeship in an opera chorus in Naples and his sensational success in America.

programme.title: Mary Berry - Love to Cook
programme.desc: Mary shares recipes that will build confidence in the kitchen, including a stir-fry hoisin chicken, a salmon and dill burger that even the most inexperienced cooks can master, and the secrets behind her best ginger biscuits. But she starts with a delectable Victoria sponge sandwich, filled with jam and topped with cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for Scotland with canapés, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. Who will be eliminated?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from Scotland compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. Who will win, and who must go home?

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the beautiful St Albans Cathedral and Abbey in Hertfordshire, antique experts Claire Rawle and James Lewis are on hand to value antiques and collectibles and take them off to auction.

programme.title: The Bridge at Remagen
programme.desc: World War II film chronicling the tense battle to hold the Ludendorff Bridge at Remagen, the only bridge still standing across the Rhine towards the end of the war. The German officer in charge of the region faces a dilemma - destroy the bridge and strand 50,000 of his own troops on the wrong side of the river, or leave the bridge intact and effectively give up the Fatherland to the advancing Allied forces.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton test Nitin Ganatra, Rev Kate Bottley and Gladiator Apollo on their knowledge of the UK. Today's tour is of Derry/Londonderry.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Set in Wyoming's spectacular Rocky Mountains, the dramatic story of two mountain lion mothers struggling to raise their kittens is helping scientists understand these elusive predators.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: TV presenters and disability rights campaigners Katie Piper and Adam Pearson attempt to turn £400 into a larger sum by flipping antiques for profit. Katie and Adam rummage in a southerly direction from Northampton to the River Thames.

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya visits Italy and Germany to explore how they're changing in the turbulent new world. She attends the Palio in Siena, joins a NATO exercise and meets a family building a nuclear bunker.

programme.title: One-Hit Wonders at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of some indelible hits by artists we hardly heard from again. Featuring Peter Sarstedt's Where Do You Go To My Lovely? a number one in 1969, a hit he never really matched; Trio's 1982 smash Da Da Da; Phyllis Nelson's 1985 lovers rock-style classic Move Closer; and the New Radicals' 1999 hit You Get What You Give. We travel through the years selecting some of your favourite number ones and a few others that came close, revealing what's happened to the one-hit wonders since.

programme.title: One-Hit Wonders at the BBC: Volume 2
programme.desc: A look into the BBC's music archives for a reminder of one-hit wonders by the Las, the Mock Turtles, Joan Jett and Kim Carnes. There are also some surprising names on the list: Don Henley, Patti Smith, Curtis Mayfield and John Denver.

programme.title: One-Hit Wonders at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of some indelible hits by artists we hardly heard from again. Featuring Peter Sarstedt's Where Do You Go To My Lovely? a number one in 1969, a hit he never really matched; Trio's 1982 smash Da Da Da; Phyllis Nelson's 1985 lovers rock-style classic Move Closer; and the New Radicals' 1999 hit You Get What You Give. We travel through the years selecting some of your favourite number ones and a few others that came close, revealing what's happened to the one-hit wonders since.

programme.title: Sound of Metal
programme.desc: Ruben, a drummer, is distraught when he begins losing his hearing. However, things take a turn when he meets Joe, who heads a community comprising of people with hearing impairment.

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: Tender dominates the headlines, sending shockwaves across the media and the government.

programme.title: Small Prophets
programme.desc: Clive is seething when a truckload of steaming horse manure is dumped in front of Michael's house. Kacey comes round to help bury the jars and asks Michael about the hoarded boxes.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: A preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Kensuke's Kingdom
programme.desc: Young Michael and his dog wash up on a remote island when a violent storm sweeps them overboard during a sailing trip. Believing he's all alone, Michael is surprised to discover a mysterious Japanese man who's lived on the island since World War II.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: With the new salmon-fishing season about to open on the River Tweed, Sammi Kinghorn and Matt Baker follow the final preparations before the first cast of the year.

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: Ian and Major head to Fort William, the biggest town in the Highlands, and the UK's self-styled outdoor capital. Ian heads up Ben Nevis to find out what the countryside has to offer and speaks to the mountain rescue team.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's judging day, and only one chef can go through to represent Scotland. Bafta-winning actress Katherine Parkinson helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: More delicious recipes from Richard Bainbridge, Julie Lin, Gaz Oakley and Shauna Froydenlund! Also, Rick Stein and Clive Myrie head out across the UK and abroad.

programme.title: The Life and Death of Colonel Blimp
programme.desc: A British epic about the life and loves of a reactionary army officer, Clive Candy VC, told episodically in flashback. From his dashing career in the Boer War of 1902, the film follows Candy through marriage during the First World War to the Blitz of 1943, when he searches London for his lost youth and loves.

programme.title: Angels One Five
programme.desc: The Battle of Britain as seen through the eyes of the operations room of a Kent fighter station during the summer of 1940. A young, inexperienced pilot has trouble settling into the routine of squadron life.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is at Huddersfield Town Hall in West Yorkshire. Experts Nick Davies and Christina Trevanion search for antiques to take to auction, and Paul delves into the area's coal mining history at the National Coal Mining Museum for England.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Ally and son Noah apply the salon treatment to go head-to-head with mainland breeders.

programme.title: Men's Six Nations
programme.desc: All the talking points and in-depth analysis from Round 4 of the Six Nations. England face a tricky trip to Rome against Italy, Ireland host Wales in Dublin and Scotland welcome France to Murrayfield.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow is at Pitzhanger Manor in Ealing, west London, where finds include a quirky 18th-century 'Harlequinade' children's flip book, a vintage trainer collection and a flying helmet from the Battle of Britain.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: With the dominant males gone, trouble looms as intruder lions close in on the Xudum pride's young cubs. Leopard Lediba searches for her missing cub after a tense encounter, and Pobe the cheetah must defend her family's food from bigger cats.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Multiple cameras follow serious crime investigations in real time, revealing the crucial role cutting edge forensic science now plays in bringing criminals to justice.

programme.title: Platoon
programme.desc: A vivid and harrowing depiction of war which follows a 19-year-old college dropout who signs up for service in Vietnam in September 1967, and finds himself plunged into the bloody chaos of life as a GI on the front line. The winner of four Academy Awards, including Best Director and Best Picture, this is an uncompromising and semi-autobiographical study of men at war from writer-director Oliver Stone.

programme.title: The Best Man Holiday
programme.desc: When four long-time friends finally reunite over the holidays they discover just how easy it is for long-forgotten rivalries and romances to be ignited.

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki, Jack, Harriet and Kit investigate the apparent suicide of a British-Chinese activist and must navigate complex international tensions.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Features a studio audience quizzing top politicians on the events of the day. The panel surrounding presenter Fiona Bruce is composed of at least one member of the three major parties.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: In this episode, the pitches are a 'nose-friendly' home composter, a GP with a medical bag redesign, a DIY drinks entrepreneur, and a husband-and-wife team with a pre-loved kitchen business.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A comprehensive compilation of the latest and the most important news stories, updates and developments from the UK and different parts of the world.

programme.title: Politics Live

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Contestants compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz show where players face a question and pick from a board of six rankable answers. Some answers are good, but only one is unbeatable.

programme.title: The Boss

programme.title: Weatherman Walking

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Isolated since the time of the dinosaurs, New Zealand's wildlife has been left to its own devices, with surprising consequences. Its ancient forests are still stalked by predators from the Jurassic era. It's also one of the most geologically active countries on Earth. From Kiwis with their giant eggs, to forest-dwelling penguins and helicopter-riding sheep dogs, meet the astonishing creatures and resilient people who must rise to the challenges of their beautiful, dramatic and demanding home.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team uncover a treasure trove of unusual objects including an original hand-written poem by W B Yeats, examples of Meissen from the earliest days of European porcelain manufacture, and a rare silver tobacco box rescued from a London dustbin.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin heads to Malvern for another valuation day where Kate Bateman and Adam Partridge lead the team of experts. Adam finds a set of playing cards with hand-written predictions, and Kate meets a mystery monk - a bronze statue that has everyone wondering what it was used for. Paul visits Whitley Court, the huge ruins of a former stately home, and uncovers its fascinating history.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Chris McCausland, Claire Richards, Adele Roberts and Olly Smith test their general knowledge skills on this week's Richard Osman's House of Games: Champions.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and unreliable guide Paul Gorton challenge Owain Wyn Evans, Greg Rutherford and Abby Cook to separate surprising truths from tall tales in Belfast.

programme.title: Global Eye

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs

At Old Castle Lachlan, he dabbles in Victorian virtual reality, courtesy of George Washington Wilson and his stereoscopic camera. At the open-air museum of Auchindrain, Paul follows Queen Victoria on the trail of poverty tourism, before having fun with the tale of MacPhunn - a man who cheated the noose.

programme.title: Mastermind

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry

programme.title: Small Prophets

programme.title: Newsnight

programme.title: BBC Weather

programme.desc: Chef Ramsay heads to Max's Bar & Grill to help the restaurant before the divorced owners' business crumbles.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Depressed that Marie and Debra can't seem to resolve their differences, Robert decides to join a support group that the family believes is a cult.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.desc: Frasier agrees to give a celebrity lecture on a cruise to Alaska and invites Niles and Martin to join him.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to The Arches on the Isle of Man where ex-demolition man Phil Routledge is proud of his super-modern B&B, which was voted the best in the Isle of Man. On arrival, his guests are blown away by the views.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the Trafford in Blackpool owned by couple Robbie and John who are proud of their traditional bucket and spade B&B. On arrival the guests are pleasantly surprised, even if previous host Phil is hell-bent on finding some dust.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit of the week is to the grand Sheepy Lodge in Warwickshire where empty-nester Julie loves to fill her manor house with people - as long as they don't order poached eggs.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the last day of the competition and the rival B&B owners meet to settle some scores and discover what they've been paid. Kicking things off is Knockaloe Beg farm on the Isle of Man, and hosts Fiona and John Anderson tackle criticisms.

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Presenters Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer mix a cookery show with a chat show, and the result is `Sunday Brunch.' Each installment finds Simon and Tim cooking in the kitchen, accompanied by that week's celebrity guests. Other segments include interviews and highlights from television and film, an expert who assists with drink tasting, a round-up of what is trendy, and the ever-popular `Sunday Brunch Playlist,' which consists of music videos chosen by the presenters and their guests. Guests have included Stacey Solomon, Lisa Faulkner, Tara Reid, Rufus Wainwright and Marina Diamandis.

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: The chapel will be unliveable for many more months, so Keith and Marj come up with a radical idea to sleep onsite a lot sooner: getting a shipping container in their car park, and transforming it into their first official finished bedroom.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: After playing mud football, Doug becomes sick and dreams of being on numerous television shows with Arthur, such as `Wheel of Fortune' and `The Young and the Restless'.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie tries to encourage Arthur to go to the weekly dance at the seniors centre. When Doug volunteers to be Arthur's carer he finds it a much more demanding task then he had anticipated.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug quits his job at IPS because he's feeling unappreciated while Carrie goes overboard with the tanning lotion.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge vie to be the subject of Lisa's `Most Interesting Person I Know' essay, but instead she chooses Professor Frink. He develops a cryptocurrency, overtaking Mr Burn's title of richest man in Springfield.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Milano Cortina 2026 Paralympics
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan rounds up the best of Day 1. Did Menna Fitzpatrick or Neil Simpson achieve a 'piste de résistance' performance in the Downhill Alpine Skiing?

programme.title: Bill Bailey's Vietnam

programme.title: The Unlikely Pilgrimage of Harold Fry
programme.desc: A seemingly unremarkable man in his 60s named Harold one day learns his old friend Queenie is dying. He embarks on a walk, only to keep going for 450 miles until he reaches Queenie's hospice, much to the despair of his wife Maureen.

programme.title: Fury
programme.desc: During World War II, a battle-hardened army sergeant leads the crew of a Sherman tank on a deadly mission behind enemy lines to strike at the heart of Nazi Germany.

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: Jonathan Ross challenges 18 brave Brits to be handcuffed to a total stranger 24/7 as they compete to win a £100,000 prize. From a bar tender and a millionaire to an alpha male and a feminist, which couple will be the first to take the cuffs off?

programme.title: Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing
programme.desc: With one pair already uncuffed and out of the running, who will be next to call it quits? The remaining pairs are still grappling with living cheek by jowl as they learn more about each other - for better and for worse.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil and Kirstie catch up with cautious first time buyers Sundeep and Rupreet in Harrow and postcode picky Hilary and Rob in Hitchin, Hertfordshire. Phil had the tricky task of widening the search beyond Hitchin. Once the couple saw what they could get for their money further out, it wasn't long before one property made it all worthwhile. They've been in their new home for just over a year.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: At his high-school reunion, Homer wins several awards including `Most Weight Gained' and `Most Improved Odour', but the Principal takes them away when he finds out that Homer never graduated. Homer vows to graduate and win them back by going to night school.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer is late picking up Bart from a school football game, Bart protests by hooking himself up with Tom, a surrogate dad from an agency. Stung by being jilted by his own son, Homer gets into a monster brawl with Tom that even makes the news.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: When a colleague of Carrie's shows an interest in Kelly, Doug and Carrie quarrel over whether or not to allow them to go on a date.

programme.title: The King of Queens

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Motorsport Mundial
programme.desc: The latest motorsport news and action from around the globe. From Superbikes to Formula 1, Motorsport Mundial has the latest action and talking points from the motorsport world.

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: Cadfael investigates when a much-hated priest is found dead after refusing to baptise a local prostitute.

programme.title: English Football League Highlights
programme.desc: Action from games across the Football League including Blackburn Rovers v Portsmouth, Hull City v Millwall, Charlton Athletic v Birmingham City, Ipswich Town v Leicester City, and Swansea City v Stoke City in the Championship.

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Brian Moore introduces highlights from September 1977. Manchester City visit West London to face Queens Park Rangers. Also in the line-up, Sheffield United take on Crystal Palace in South London and a Midlands derby sees Wolverhampton Wanderers face West Bromwich Albion.

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Brian Moore presents action from fixtures in the 1984/85 football season - highlights include an in-form Sunderland against Queens Park Rangers, promotion hunters Brighton and Hove Albion facing Manchester City, and goals galore in Scotland as Dundee United play St Mirren.

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Discover how Maldon Sea Salt is made according to traditional methods on the shores of the Blackwater in Essex. Also, take a look behind the scenes at the Brompton bike factory in London, the UK's biggest bicycle manufacturer.

programme.title: Live: UK Open Darts
programme.desc: Pete Graves hosts alongside Wayne Mardle, Glen Durrant, and Chris Mason as the road to glory intensifies at the 2026 Ladbrokes UK Open at Butlin's Minehead - with the quarter-finalists going head-to-head for a place in the semi-finals of darts' FA Cup, a draw will determine the final match-ups.

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Lucas and Adam come across a truly incredible military collection, and Lucas picks some quality furniture.

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry, Allen and Guy finally take possession of the Buick Roadmaster. After a little tinkering they cruise down to a classic car show in Newbury, but being the Junketeers can't help themselves but do a little wheeling and dealing.

programme.title: Live: UK Open Darts
programme.desc: Join Pete Graves, Wayne Mardle, Glen Durrant and Chris Mason for a dramatic conclusion to the 2026 Ladbrokes UK Open at Butlin's Minehead - the semi-finals set the stage for the final showdown as the four remaining stars battle to lift the trophy and follow Luke Littler as champion in one of darts' biggest events.

programme.title: English Football League Highlights
programme.desc: Action from games across the Football League including Blackburn Rovers v Portsmouth, Hull City v Millwall, Charlton Athletic v Birmingham City, Ipswich Town v Leicester City, and Swansea City v Stoke City in the Championship.

programme.title: Black Sails
programme.desc: Flint and Gates seek additional help in their search for the Urca d'Lima, Silver and Billy tackle a morale problem, Eleanor is impressed by Vane, and Gates is promoted.

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: When suspicions mount that a pair of CID officers might be involved in bribery, Regan is sent undercover as an ex-con to investigate whether the corruption allegations are true.

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: A daily delivery of bespoke Mindfulness content designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Presenting the latest products live on television that can be ordered directly. The product range extends from vacuum cleaners and bath towels to sports equipment.

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Brian Moore hosts action from November 1977. West Ham United take on West Bromwich Albion, Leeds United host Manchester City and Nottingham Forest face Manchester United.

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Brian Moore presents football action from November 1977 as Second Division promotion hunters Tottenham and Brighton face off at White Hart Lane. Plus, action from the First Division where Manchester United take on Norwich.

programme.title: Magnum, P.I.
programme.desc: While in London, Magnum discovers an old buddy was killed in a hit-and-run accident.

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Internal Affairs and a hostile district attorney investigate Kojak when a thief claims the lieutenant took a bribe.

programme.title: Boon
programme.desc: Ken is hired by an environmental group to investigate water pollution.

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Following the death of one of London's leading villains, Regan gets involved in gangland warfare, while a family dispute over the dubious inheritance risks complicating matters.

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: American mobster plans to eliminate governor of the Bank of England.

programme.title: Magnum, P.I.
programme.desc: Magnum tries to make sense of the discrepancies surrounding his friend's death.

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A young man must protect a frame-up arranged by his father (Tige Andrews) to avenge his paralysed brother.

programme.title: Boon
programme.desc: Ken's back in business with a new boat and a new name for the company.

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: After an ex-con at a stag do drunkenly reveals that he got away with an earlier robbery, Regan suspects that he is being fitted up to get his daughter's equally criminal boyfriend out of the picture.

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A scheming builder arranges for a group of heavies to intimidate a car dealer into selling his land.

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald hit Australia's back roads and head to a sprawling property owned by Laurie, a larrikin demolisher and collector whose language makes even the pickers blush.

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Motorcycle guru Henry Cole is in the Yorkshire Dales taking in the delights of some of the UK's best motorcycling roads. He also tells the story of Indian - one of America's most venerable motorbike brands, and begins the rebuild of his BSA Goldstar.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Lisa forms a convoy with George and Tim, but is frustrated by their slow pace. Meanwhile, Alex hopes to pull ahead in the race to complete more journeys, Hugh dodges caribou as he returns from his suspension and Jack has a tank full of fluid to deliver to Coldfoot.

programme.title: Kill Bill: Vol. 1
programme.desc: Waking from a coma, a female contract killer wreaks revenge on the assassins who tried to kill her. Known as "The Bride", because she was gunned down on her wedding day, the as yet nameless woman awakens to discover she is no longer pregnant. She goes on a killing spree, taking out one by one the members of her old team of assassins who killed her baby.
